content.problemStatement:
Given a text `T` of length `n` and a pattern `P` of length `m`, the task is to find all occurrences of the pattern `P` in the text `T`. The approach to solving this problem will be the **Naive String Matching Algorithm**.

In this approach, we will compare the pattern `P` with every substring of `T` that has the same length as `P`. If a match is found, record the starting index of the substring in `T`.

Input:
The first line contains the string `T` (the text) of length `n` containing only lowercase English letters.
The second line contains the string `P` (the pattern) of length `m` containing only lowercase English letters.
Output:
The output should be the starting indices (0-based) of all occurrences of the pattern `P` in the text `T`. If no match is found, output should be `-1`.
constraints: The text and pattern consist of only lowercase English letters.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
At the index 0 and 9 we have the pattern abc

code.Python3:
def computeLPSArray(pattern):
    m = len(pattern)
    lps = [0] * m  # lps array to store the longest prefix suffix lengths
    length = 0  # Length of the previous longest prefix suffix
    i = 1
    while i < m:
        if pattern[i] == pattern[length]:
            length += 1
            lps[i] = length
            i += 1
        else:
            if length != 0:
                length = lps[length - 1]  # Use the previously computed lps value
            else:
                lps[i] = 0
                i += 1
    return lps

def KMPSearch(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    
    # Compute LPS array for the pattern
    lps = computeLPSArray(pattern)
    
    i = 0  # Index for the text
    j = 0  # Index for the pattern
    flag = 0  # To track if we found any match

    while i < n:
        if pattern[j] == text[i]:
            i += 1
            j += 1

        if j == m:  # If the entire pattern is found
            print(i - j, end=" ")  # Pattern found, print the index
            j = lps[j - 1]  # Reset pattern index based on lps
            flag = 1
        elif i < n and pattern[j] != text[i]:
            if j != 0:
                j = lps[j - 1]  # Use the lps to skip characters
            else:
                i += 1

    if flag == 0:
        print(-1)  # If no match was found, print -1

# Read input
text = input().strip()
pattern = input().strip()

# Perform KMP search
KMPSearch(text, pattern)
